candidates.text: I worked nights at Woolies during uni. There were always stacks of perfectly good rotisserie chickens and fresh bakery items left at closing. At first, night staff could take them home free or heavily discounted. Then head office banned it - no markdowns, no staff takeaways. Every night I’d throw 20+ good chickens and piles of bread in the bin. One coworker took a couple home for her kids. She got caught and was fired. They wouldn’t even donate to charities or food banks.
